summary: The conjecture in its full generality was disproved by Kochol (2009), who constructed snarks with polyhedral embeddings in orientable surfaces of genus $\geq 5$, showing that the dual of a simple loopless triangulation of such a surface need not be 3-edge-colorable. For the torus (genus 1), the conjecture was fully confirmed in 2025 by Inoue, Kawarabayashi, Miyashita, Mohar, and Sonobe, who characterized all non-3-edge-colorable toroidal cubic graphs as exactly the Petersen-like graphs. The conjecture remains open for orientable surfaces of genera 2, 3, and 4.

since_posted.title: Grünbaum Colorings of Toroidal Triangulations
since_posted.authors: Michael O. Albertson, Hannah Alpert, sarah-marie belcastro, Ruth Haas
since_posted.claim: Proves Grünbaum's conjecture for toroidal triangulations G with chi(G) != 5: if G triangulates the torus and has chromatic number different from 5, then there exists a 3-edge-coloring of G such that every facial triangle receives all three colors.

since_posted.title: Polyhedral embeddings of snarks in orientable surfaces
since_posted.venue: Proceedings of the American Mathematical Society
since_posted.claim: Shows that snarks have polyhedral embeddings in orientable surfaces of sufficiently large genus, giving simple loopless triangulations of orientable surfaces whose duals are not 3-edge-colorable and thereby disproving Grünbaum's conjecture in full generality.

since_posted.title: Three-edge-coloring (Tait coloring) cubic graphs on the torus: A proof of Grünbaum's conjecture
since_posted.authors: Yuta Inoue, Ken-ichi Kawarabayashi, Atsuyuki Miyashita, Bojan Mohar, Tomohiro Sonobe
since_posted.claim: Proves that a 2-connected cubic multigraph embedded in the torus fails to be 3-edge-colorable if and only if it is Petersen-like (obtained from Petersen graph dot products), thereby fully confirming Grünbaum's conjecture for the torus: every polyhedral embedding in the torus yields a 3-edge-colorable dual.

triangulation dual 3-edge-coloring orientable surface face-width snark recent progress
Kochol "3-regular non 3-edge-colorable" polyhedral embedding orientable surface
notes: The primary disproof is Kochol (2009), 'Polyhedral embeddings of snarks in orientable surfaces,' Proc. AMS 137:1613-1619, DOI 10.1090/S0002-9939-08-09698-6; the AMS page is now directly verified and states that the paper gives counterexamples to Grünbaum's conjecture. A companion conference paper appeared in GD 2008, LNCS 5417, ch. 31. The 2024 projective-plane companion paper arXiv:2405.16586 (same author team as the torus paper) is related but concerns a non-orientable surface and is not included in since_posted. Status for genus 2, 3, 4 surfaces remains open per multiple search results.
